name: Node TypeScript Yargs CLI
description: Scaffold a Node.js CLI application using TypeScript and yargs, ready to build and publish to npm.
author: Projgen Node CLI Template

variables.name: projectName
variables.message: Folder name for the project (will be created in the current directory)

variables.name: cliCommand
variables.message: CLI command name (what users will run in their terminal)

variables.name: description
variables.default: A Node.js CLI built with TypeScript and yargs

steps.name: Create project directory
steps.description: Create the project folder in the current working directory.

steps.name: npm init
steps.description: Initialize package.json using npm init -y.

steps.name: Set package name
steps.description: Set package.json name to the chosen packageName.

steps.name: Set description
steps.description: Set package description.

steps.name: Set author
steps.description: Set package author.

steps.name: Set license
steps.description: Use MIT license by default.

steps.name: Set module type
steps.description: Use ESM module type for Node.

steps.name: Set main entry
steps.description: Point main to dist/index.js.

steps.name: Set bin mapping
steps.description: Expose the CLI command via the bin field.

steps.name: Set files field
steps.description: Only publish the dist directory.

steps.name: Set scripts: build
steps.description: Add TypeScript build script.

steps.name: Set scripts: dev
steps.description: Add dev script using tsx on the CLI entrypoint.

steps.name: Set scripts: start
steps.description: Run built CLI from dist.

steps.name: Set scripts: test
steps.description: Run tests with Vitest.

steps.name: Set scripts: lint
steps.description: Run ESLint on TypeScript files.

steps.name: Set keywords
steps.description: Add useful keywords for npm search.

steps.name: Install runtime dependencies
steps.description: Install yargs for CLI argument parsing.

steps.name: Install dev dependencies
steps.description: Install TypeScript, tsx, types, Vitest and ESLint.

steps.name: tsc --init
steps.description: Generate a base tsconfig.json using the TypeScript compiler.

steps.name: Configure tsconfig: target
steps.description: Set TS target to ES2020.

steps.name: Configure tsconfig: module
steps.description: Use NodeNext module resolution for modern Node ESM.

steps.name: Configure tsconfig: moduleResolution
steps.description: Align moduleResolution with NodeNext.

steps.name: Configure tsconfig: rootDir/outDir
steps.description: Output compiled JS into dist from src.

steps.name: Configure tsconfig: outDir
steps.description: Set outDir to dist.

steps.name: Configure tsconfig: strict flags
steps.description: Enable strict mode and common quality-of-life compiler options.

steps.name: Configure tsconfig: esModuleInterop
steps.description: Enable esModuleInterop for CJS/ESM compatibility.

steps.name: Configure tsconfig: resolveJsonModule
steps.description: Allow importing JSON modules.

steps.name: Configure tsconfig: casing and skipLibCheck
steps.description: Consistent file casing and skip library checks.

steps.name: Configure tsconfig: skipLibCheck
steps.description: Skip library type checking for faster builds.

steps.name: Configure tsconfig: preserveShebang
steps.description: Preserve shebang in compiled CLI output.

steps.name: Write .gitignore
steps.description: Fetch Node.gitignore from GitHub’s official gitignore repository.

steps.name: ESLint init
steps.description: Run ESLint’s init command to create a config and install ESLint-related deps.

steps.name: Vitest init
steps.description: Run Vitest init to scaffold test configuration.

steps.name: Create README.md
steps.description: Basic README with usage instructions.
steps.content:
# {{projectName}}

> {{description}}

## Development

```bash
npm install
npm run dev
```

## Build

```bash
npm run build
```

## Usage

After building, you can run the CLI using node directly:

```bash
node dist/cli.js --help
```

Or, after publishing to npm and installing globally:

```bash
{{cliCommand}} --help
```

steps.name: Create src/index.ts
steps.description: Library entry with a simple greet helper.

steps.name: Create src/cli.ts
steps.description: CLI entrypoint using yargs, wired to the greet helper.

steps.name: Initialize git
steps.description: Initialize a new git repository in the project.
